articles.title: “No Further Orders Necessary”: Supreme Court Dismisses TMC Plea On Centre-Appointed Counting Officers In Bengal Elections
articles.summary: The Supreme Court dismissed a plea by the Trinamool Congress challenging the Election Commission of India's (ECI) decision to appoint central government and central PSU employees as counting officers for West Bengal's elections. The Court upheld the ECI's circular mandating a random selection of counting personnel from central and state government employees, stating no further orders were necessary. The ECI had assured the Court that the April 13, 2026 circular would be followed strictly.

articles.title: Ease in Tax Litigation under Income-tax Act, 2025: A Structural Analysis of Procedural Reforms
articles.summary: The article analyzes proposed reforms to the Income-tax Act, 2025, aimed at simplifying tax litigation by merging multiple proceedings into a single unified process. This change seeks to reduce duplication and streamline procedures under the new tax regime. The reforms are structural and procedural, focusing on easing litigation rather than eliminating it entirely. The analysis is based on the government's initiative to modernize tax dispute resolution mechanisms.
articles.raw_summary: The issue concerns fragmented litigation under the earlier tax regime. The new law integrates multiple proceedings into a unified process, reducing duplication. The key takeaway is that litigation is streamlined rather than eliminated. articles.summary: The Telangana High Court granted relief in a GST dispute by requiring a 10% pre-deposit and staying coercive recovery actions until the GST Tribunal resolves the case. The ruling impacts businesses and tax authorities by setting conditions for pending disputes. The decision was made in response to a pending GST dispute where the court intervened to prevent immediate recovery while the matter is adjudicated.

articles.title: Gujarat High Court Upholds State Law On Acquisition Of Land For Water & Gas Pipelines, Finds No Conflict With Central Law
articles.summary: The Gujarat High Court has held that state enactment Gujarat Water & Gas Pipelines (Acquisition of Right of User in Land) Act 2000 is not repugnant to the central enactment Petroleum and Minerals Pipelines (Acquisition of Right of User in Land) Act, 1962 finding that the provisions of the two laws are pari-materia. The court was considering whether 2000 Act is within the legislative competence of the State legislature and if its competence can be traced to Entry 42 of List III (Concurrent List)
articles.raw_summary: The Gujarat High Court has held that state enactment Gujarat Water & Gas Pipelines (Acquisition of Right of User in Land) Act 2000 is not repugnant to the central enactment Petroleum and Minerals Pipelines (Acquisition of Right of User in Land) Act, 1962 finding that the provisions of the two laws are pari-materia. The court was considering whether 2000 Act is within the legislative competence of the State legislature and if its competence can be traced to Entry 42 of List III (Concurrent List)

articles.title: Section 14 under IBC (Amendment) Act, 2026: Scope of Moratorium & Decriminalising Contraventions
articles.summary: The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC) Amendment Act, 2026, clarifies that guarantors cannot initiate recovery against corporate debtors during the Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (CIRP). This ensures all claims are resolved collectively within the insolvency framework. The amendment also decriminalizes certain contraventions, streamlining the insolvency process. The changes aim to strengthen the IBC's effectiveness in resolving corporate distress.
articles.raw_summary: The amendment clarifies that guarantors cannot initiate recovery against the corporate debtor during CIRP. It ensures all claims are resolved collectively within the insolvency process. The post Section 14 under IBC (Amendment) Act, 2026: Scope of Moratorium   Decriminalising Contraventions appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Section 12A under IBC (Amendment) Act, 2026: Strict Timeline for CIRP Withdrawal
articles.summary: The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC) Amendment Act, 2026 introduces stricter timelines for withdrawing Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (CIRP) applications. Withdrawals are now permitted only between the constitution of the Committee of Creditors (CoC) and the invitation of resolution plans. The amendment aims to enforce procedural discipline and prevent disruptions in insolvency proceedings.
articles.raw_summary: The amendment limits withdrawal only between CoC constitution and invitation of resolution plans. It ensures procedural discipline and avoids disruption of insolvency proceedings. The post Section 12A under IBC (Amendment) Act, 2026: Strict Timeline for CIRP Withdrawal appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: IBC (Amendment) Act, 2026: Faster Timelines and a Creditor-Driven Regime
articles.summary: The IBC (Amendment) Act, 2026 introduces faster timelines for insolvency resolution by mandating time-bound decisions by the National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT). articles.title: When Myanmar Exported Federalism
articles.summary: After World War II, Myanmar (then Burma) helped draft a federal constitution for Ethiopia and Eritrea while simultaneously fighting a civil war with ethnic minorities demanding federalism at home. This historical case highlights the complexities of federal governance and ethnic minority rights, which remain relevant to discussions on federalism and governance in India and globally.
articles.raw_summary: After World War II, Burma helped design a federal constitution for Ethiopia and Eritrea, while fighting a civil war with ethnic minorities demanding the same at home.

articles.title: Telangana HC Allows GST Revocation Despite Time-Bar, Cites Accountant’s Default
articles.summary: The Telangana High Court allowed a taxpayer to seek GST revocation despite missing the statutory deadline due to an accountant's default in non-filing returns. The court permitted manual filing and directed authorities to consider such applications on merits rather than rejecting them solely for procedural delays. This ruling impacts how tax authorities handle late GST revocation requests, emphasizing fairness over strict timelines.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether a taxpayer could seek revocation after missing the statutory deadline due to non-filing of returns. The court allowed manual filing, holding that authorities should consider such applications on merits despite procedural delay. articles.title: GST Parallel Proceedings Valid if Subject Matter Differs: Telangana HC
articles.summary: The Telangana High Court ruled that GST parallel proceedings are valid if the subject matter differs, even for the same tax period. The court held that separate adjudication orders for short payment and excess Input Tax Credit (ITC) do not violate the bar on overlapping actions under GST law. This decision clarifies the legal framework for tax adjudication and may influence future GST enforcement and compliance practices.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether two adjudication orders for the same tax period constituted overlapping proceedings under GST law. The court held that proceedings on different issues—short payment vs. excess ITC—do not violate the bar on parallel actions. articles.title: Tax Treatment of Dealer Benefits Under Section 194R & Income Tax Act 2025
articles.summary: The article explains that under the Income Tax Act, 2025, dealer incentives like tour packages are now taxable as business income. articles.raw_summary: Dealer incentives like tour packages are taxable as business income under the new law. If TDS is borne by the payer, it must be added as additional income through grossing up. articles.summary: The Gujarat High Court upheld the denial of Input Tax Credit (ITC) to a purchaser when the supplier failed to deposit GST to the government. The ruling clarifies that ITC eligibility depends on the supplier's actual tax payment, not just the purchaser's compliance. This decision reinforces the government's stance on preventing tax evasion through ITC claims. The case involved ITC and highlights the legal consequences of supplier defaults under GST law.
articles.raw_summary: The Court examined whether ITC can be denied when the supplier fails to deposit tax. It upheld the provision, ruling that ITC depends on actual tax payment to the Government. The key takeaway is that purchaser compliance alone is insufficient without supplier tax remittance. articles.title: 870-Day Delay Not Condoned: ITAT Refuses Relief, Calls Out Negligence & “No Sufficient Cause”
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) dismissed appeals due to an 870-day delay in filing, citing negligence and absence of a credible explanation. The tribunal ruled that no sufficient cause was shown to condone the delay, setting a precedent for strict adherence to procedural timelines in tax appeals. articles.title: GST Registration Cancellation Invalid Due to Vague Notice & Missing Reasons: P&H HC
articles.summary: The Punjab & Haryana High Court invalidated the retrospective cancellation of a GST registration due to a vague show cause notice lacking clear reasons and supporting documents. The court ruled that this violated natural justice principles and quashed the cancellation, directing fresh proceedings to be conducted as per legal requirements. The decision underscores the importance of procedural fairness in tax administration.
articles.raw_summary: The court held that a show cause notice lacking clear reasons and supporting documents violates natural justice. It quashed the retrospective cancellation, allowing fresh proceedings as per law. GST Not Applicable on Arbitral Damages Without Supply Element: Bombay HC
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court ruled that Goods and Services Tax (GST) is not applicable to arbitral damages or settlement terms unless there is a 'supply' element under GST law. The court quashed an Integrated Goods and Services Tax (IGST) demand, clarifying that such damages do not constitute consideration for supply. The ruling impacts tax assessments for disputes resolved through arbitration in India.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether arbitral award damages and settlement terms constitute “supply” under GST law. The Court held they do not amount to consideration for supply, and quashed the IGST demand. articles.title: GST Not Applicable on Solar Power Supply Due to Exemption for Electrical Energy: AAR Tamilnadu
articles.summary: The Tamil Nadu Authority for Advance Ruling (AAR) ruled that electricity supplied through solar power plants is exempt from GST, as electrical energy is considered an exempt good. The ruling clarifies that no GST registration is required when only exempt supplies are involved. This decision impacts businesses in the solar energy sector and aligns with existing tax exemptions for electricity.
articles.raw_summary: The issue concerned GST liability on electricity supplied through a solar power plant. The AAR held that electrical energy is exempt from GST and no registration is required when only exempt goods are supplied. articles.title: Employee Transport Recovery Not Taxable as It Is Not in Course of Business: AAR Tamilnadu
articles.summary: The Authority for Advance Ruling (AAR) in Tamil Nadu ruled that employee transport recovery by employers is not taxable under GST because it is considered a welfare activity, not a business supply. The nominal amount recovered from employees for transport does not attract GST. This decision clarifies the tax treatment of employee welfare measures and impacts how businesses structure such arrangements.
articles.raw_summary: The authority held that arranging transport for employees is a welfare activity, not a business supply. Nominal recovery from employees does not attract GST. articles.title: Personal Liberty Cannot Be Lightly Curtailed In Cases Coloured By Political Rivalry: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that personal liberty cannot be lightly curtailed in cases where allegations appear politically motivated. The case involved a politically sensitive dispute where arrest was questioned. The Court emphasized that fundamental rights must be protected even in contentious political contexts. This ruling reinforces judicial safeguards against arbitrary detention in politically charged scenarios.

The move follows regional tensions and could impact India's trade routes, particularly for oil and energy imports.

articles.title: The Plant Fibre Sector Keeps Chasing Visibility When It Needs Markets
articles.summary: The plant fibre sector in India is gaining attention as industries seek bio-based materials with lower environmental impact. However, output remains low due to fragmented supply chains, weak processing capacity, and uncertain demand. A FIBRAL report suggests that diversification will require standards, investment, and market architecture, not just increased fibre production. The sector's struggles highlight potential areas for policy intervention to boost sustainable alternatives.
articles.raw_summary: As industries seek bio-based materials with lower environmental impact, alternative plant fibres are drawing new policy and commercial attention. Yet output remains marginal and forecast to contract, constrained by fragmented chains, weak processing capacity and uncertain offtake. A FIBRAL report argues diversification will depend on standards, investment and market architecture, not fibre discovery alone at scale.

articles.title: Sale Deeds Invalid as Power of Attorney Not Proved Through Proper Evidence: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that sale deeds are invalid if the authority to sell via a power of attorney is not proven through proper legal evidence. The Court held that a photocopy of a power of attorney cannot be relied upon without meeting the legal requirements for secondary evidence. This decision impacts property transactions where sale authority is disputed.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that a photocopy of a power of attorney cannot be relied upon without fulfilling legal requirements for secondary evidence. As the authority to sell was not established, the sale deeds were declared invalid. Earlier court rulings often blocked terminations if the fetus was healthy, but this order deviates from precedent. The decision highlights the conflict between a survivor's choice and the unborn baby's right to life. The case underscores the need for clearer legal frameworks on abortion rights in India.

articles.title: Govt reduces windfall gains tax on diesel, jet fuel
articles.summary: The Indian government has reduced the Special Additional Excise Duty (SAED) on diesel exports from ₹55.5 per litre to ₹23 per litre and on aviation turbine fuel from ₹42 per litre to ₹33 per litre. This move aims to ease export costs for these fuels. The decision is part of the central government's fiscal policy adjustments to address windfall gains in the energy sector.
articles.raw_summary: The Special Additional Excise Duty (SAED) on export of diesel has been reduced to ₹23 per litre from ₹55.5 per litre, and aviation turbine fuel to ₹33 per litre from ₹42 per litre.

articles.title: Karnataka police told not to summon women at night; enforce KSHRC guidelines
articles.summary: The Karnataka Police have been instructed not to summon women at night for questioning, except in emergencies where a woman police officer must be present. This directive follows guidelines from the Karnataka State Human Rights Commission (KSHRC). The move aims to protect women's safety and ensure proper procedures are followed during nighttime interrogations.

articles.title: West Bengal polls: Election Commission scraps Falta election, orders repolling on May 21
articles.summary: The Election Commission of India cancelled polling in West Bengal's Falta constituency due to widespread EVM tampering and ordered repolling on May 21. Allegations of threats against villagers and the arrest of a candidate's aides have heightened tensions. The decision follows concerns over electoral integrity and could impact the state's government formation process.

A repoll is set for May 21, making it a unique post-result election. Allegations of threats against villagers and the arrest of a candidate's aides have heightened tensions, focusing attention on the remaining seats to decide the state's next government.

articles.title: Private Premises Can’t Be Turned Into Unregulated Congregational Space: Allahabad HC
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court ruled that private premises cannot be used for unregulated religious gatherings if they affect public order. While private worship is permitted, organized gatherings are subject to legal restrictions. The court held that such use can be regulated under existing laws. This decision underscores the balance between religious freedom and public order in India.
articles.raw_summary: The issue involved seeking permission for regular religious gatherings at private premises. The Court held that such use is not protected if it affects public order and can be regulated. The key takeaway is that private worship is allowed, but organized gatherings are subject to legal restrictions. articles.title: India invokes WTO peace clause for FY25 rice subsidies for farmers
articles.summary: India has once again utilized the World Trade Organisation peace clause. This is the seventh time the country has invoked this provision. This represents about 11.85% of the total value of rice production.

articles.title: UP introduces 10-year ‘warranty’ model for rural water schemes under Jal Jeevan Mission
articles.summary: The Uttar Pradesh government has launched a 10-year 'warranty' model for rural water schemes under the central Jal Jeevan Mission. Under this framework, construction agencies will be responsible for operating and maintaining water supply infrastructure for a decade. This aims to ensure long-term sustainability of rural water supply systems. The initiative is part of broader efforts to improve water access in rural areas.
articles.raw_summary: Under the new framework, construction agencies will remain responsible for operating and maintaining water supply infrastructure for a decade.

articles.title: 100% FDI allowed in insurance sector under automatic route, inflows for LIC capped at 20%
articles.summary: The central government has approved 100% Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) in insurance companies through the automatic route, allowing full foreign ownership. This decision aligns with the Sabka Bima Sabki Raksha Act, 2025, and requires approval from the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI). However, Life Insurance Corporation (LIC) remains an exception with a 20% FDI cap. The move aims to boost foreign participation in India's insurance sector.
articles.raw_summary: The central government has announced 100% Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) in insurance companies via the automatic route, enabling full foreign ownership. This move, aligning with the Sabka Bima Sabki Raksha Act, 2025, aims to boost foreign participation, with IRDAI approval required. LIC remains an exception with a 20% FDI limit.

The case reflects broader global efforts to regulate digital platforms and ensure child safety in the online space.

articles.title: Japan’s Takaichi to Revamp Indo-Pacific Vision in Vietnam Speech
articles.summary: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi will deliver a speech in Vietnam outlining her vision for the Indo-Pacific region. The speech aims to strengthen economic and security ties across Asia, potentially serving as a counterbalance to China's growing influence. This initiative could impact India's strategic interests in the region, given its own concerns about China's assertiveness and the importance of the Indo-Pacific for Indian trade and security.
articles.raw_summary: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi on Saturday will use a speech in Vietnam to lay out her vision for the Indo-Pacific, as she seeks to bolster economic and security ties across the region in a move that could counter an increasingly assertive China.

articles.summary: The government raised commercial LPG prices significantly while keeping diesel, petrol, domestic LPG, and jet fuel rates unchanged. This move aims to balance energy pricing across industry segments while protecting mass consumers from further cost burdens. The decision reflects a policy-driven effort to manage fuel costs without overburdening households or critical sectors.

articles.title: Wheat output may miss expectations as weather risks rise: ITC’s Somnath Chatterjee
articles.summary: Somnath Chatterjee, Chief Product Officer of ITC’s Food Division, warns that India’s wheat production may fall short of expectations due to rising weather risks, including El Niño, fertiliser shortages, and water scarcity. He notes that while the government has taken steps like easing procurement rules to support farmers, larger systemic challenges could tighten wheat supplies in the next crop cycle. The concerns highlight potential food security risks that may require policy interventions.
articles.raw_summary: Somnath Chatterjee, CPO–Food Division at ITC, highlights government steps such as relaxed procurement norms to support farmers, but flags larger risks ahead—from El Niño to fertiliser supply and water availability—which could impact the next crop cycle and keep wheat supplies tight.

articles.title: FDI inflows set to top $90 billion in FY26, says DPIIT Secretary Bhatia
articles.summary: Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) inflows into India are projected to exceed $90 billion in the fiscal year 2025-26, according to DPIIT Secretary Rajesh Kumar Singh Bhatia. As of February, FDI inflows have already reached $88 billion, driven by economic reforms, progress on free trade agreements, and strong growth. The government attributes this momentum to policy measures aimed at attracting investment.

articles.title: No Option for 18% GST as Outdoor Catering Falls Under Mandatory 5% Entry: AAR Tamilnadu
articles.summary: The Tamil Nadu Appellate Authority for Advance Ruling (AAR) ruled that outdoor catering services must be taxed at a mandatory 5% GST rate without input tax credit (ITC), leaving no option for taxpayers to choose the higher 18% rate. This decision clarifies the GST classification for outdoor catering, impacting businesses and tax compliance. The ruling was issued in response to a taxpayer query on whether concessional and standard GST rates could be selected.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether taxpayers could choose between concessional and standard GST rates. The AAR held that once classified as outdoor catering, the 5% rate without ITC is mandatory. articles.summary: India has eased foreign investment rules, allowing up to 100% FDI in insurance and relaxing norms for investments from neighbouring countries. These changes aim to boost capital inflows amidst a weakening rupee.

articles.title: The world wants to eliminate cervical cancer - how Australian scientists led the way
articles.summary: Australia has recorded no new cervical cancer cases in women under 25 for the first time, achieved by vaccinating both young men and women. This success is attributed to a national vaccination program targeting the human papillomavirus (HPV), which causes most cervical cancers. The approach could inform global health policies, including those in India, where cervical cancer remains a significant public health concern.
articles.raw_summary: For the first time, Australia has recorded no new cervical cancer diagnoses in women under 25. To get there, they vaccinated young men.

The case highlights potential misuse of legal processes in political rivalries.

articles.summary: The Rushikulya rookery in Odisha is a critical nesting site for olive ridley sea turtles, with nearly 700,000 turtles arriving in February 2025 for mass nesting. Plastic waste and discarded fishing nets (ghost nets) pose severe threats to hatchlings, risking their survival. The article underscores the urgent need for conservation efforts to protect this endangered species and its habitat.
articles.raw_summary: The Rushikulya rookery in Odisha’s Ganjam district is among the most important turtle rookeries in the world. Every year, hundreds of thousands of olive ridley sea turtles (Lepidochelys olivacea) converge here to lay eggs in a synchronous mass nesting event or arribada, with almost 700,000 olive ridleys coming ashore to lay eggs in February 2025 alone. articles.title: Bombay HC says WhatsApp can’t wait for court orders to remove scam groups
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court ruled that WhatsApp cannot wait for court orders to remove scam groups from its platform. The ruling highlights the legal obligations of digital platforms in addressing fraudulent content.

articles.raw_summary: YourStory presents the daily news roundup from the Indian startup ecosystem and beyond. Here's the roundup for Thursday , April 30, 2026.

articles.title: India's total power consumption grows by 4% to 154 billion units in April
articles.summary: Peak power demand reached a record 256.11 GW. Experts predict further increases in power demand from May onwards due to a harsh summer. Monsoon is expected to arrive in June.
articles.raw_summary: Peak power demand reached a record 256.11 GW. Experts predict further increases in power demand from May onwards due to a harsh summer. Monsoon is expected to arrive in June.

articles.title: Adani Enterprises posts ₹220.71 crore loss in Q4 FY26
articles.summary: The company’s revenue from operations grew 20.30 per cent YoY to ₹32,439.31 crore in Q4 FY26, versus ₹26,965.86 crore in the corresponding quarter last year.
articles.raw_summary: The company’s revenue from operations grew 20.30 per cent YoY to ₹32,439.31 crore in Q4 FY26, versus ₹26,965.86 crore in the corresponding quarter last year.

articles.title: After Trump dismissed another proposal, Iran says ‘ball is in US’ court', ready for ‘negotiated settlement or war’
articles.summary: Iran has proposed a deal to the US that would reopen shipping in the Strait of Hormuz and end the US blockade, with talks on Iran's nuclear program to follow. However, US President Trump has dismissed this proposal. Iran states the ball is now in the US' court and is prepared for either a negotiated settlement or war. The situation could disrupt global oil supplies, affecting India's energy imports.
articles.raw_summary: An Iranian proposal that Trump has so far rejected would open shipping in the Hormuz & end the US blockade, leaving talks on Iran’s nuclear programme for later.

articles.title: War and your SIPs: Stay, pause or exit?
articles.summary: Indian equities experienced significant volatility as geopolitical tensions impacted markets, leading to a weaker rupee and investor caution. While fear drives knee-jerk reactions, the core design of Systematic Investment Plans (SIPs) is built to navigate these phases, turning market swings into a potential long-term advantage for disciplined investors.
articles.raw_summary: Indian equities experienced significant volatility as geopolitical tensions impacted markets, leading to a weaker rupee and investor caution. While fear drives knee-jerk reactions, the core design of Systematic Investment Plans (SIPs) is built to navigate these phases, turning market swings into a potential long-term advantage for disciplined investors.

articles.title: Repoll today at 15 booths of West Bengal's South 24 Parganas
articles.summary: The Election Commission (EC) has ordered repolls today at 15 polling booths in West Bengal's South 24 Parganas after declaring the original polls void. The decision follows reports from Returning Officers and Observers. This action ensures fair electoral processes in the affected areas.
articles.raw_summary: According to the EC's order, the polls at the affected booths were declared void based on reports from Returning Officers (ROs) and Observers.

articles.title: Canada intelligence flags Khalistani extremists as ongoing national security threat
articles.summary: Canada's intelligence agency (CSIS) has identified Khalistani extremists as an ongoing national security threat. The group is accused of exploiting Canadian institutions and community ties to raise funds, some of which are allegedly diverted to support violent activities. This raises concerns for India due to the transnational nature of extremist networks and their potential impact on bilateral relations.
articles.raw_summary: CSIS says some CBKEs exploit Canadian institutions and community ties to raise funds, diverting money from unsuspecting donors to support violent activities.

articles.title: Iran war and its discontents
articles.summary: The West Asia crisis is causing widespread harm, affecting global stability and economies. The article argues that durable peace requires broader negotiations involving multiple stakeholders and credible agencies. The situation has potential implications for India's energy imports, regional security, and diplomatic engagements.
articles.raw_summary: The West Asia crisis is hurting everyone. Durable peace needs a broader conversation involving multiple parties and credible agencies

articles.title: Two FIRs against Sandeep Pathak in Punjab, BJP calls it 'vendetta politics': What we know
articles.summary: Sandeep Pathak was seen leaving his Delhi residence. When approached for a response, the Rajya Sabha MP did not engage and left in his car.
articles.raw_summary: Sandeep Pathak was seen leaving his Delhi residence. When approached for a response, the Rajya Sabha MP did not engage and left in his car.

articles.title: ‘Death was right in front of us’: Survivors recall chaos on Jabalpur boat that killed 11 in Madhya Pradesh
articles.summary: A cruise boat carrying over 40 passengers capsized in Madhya Pradesh's Narmada reservoir due to strong winds and rough waters, killing 11 people. Survivors described chaos as the boat overturned, throwing passengers into the water. The incident raises questions about safety regulations for water transport and tourism infrastructure in the state. The boat was operating under local authorities' jurisdiction.
articles.raw_summary: A cruise carrying more than 40 passengers overturned during strong winds and rough water conditions and several people were thrown into the Narmada reservoir.

articles.title: Great Nicobar project key for India’s economic growth and military footprint, its opposition will benefit China: Defence veterans
articles.summary: Defence veterans, including former IAF chief R K S Bhadauria, strongly back the Greater Nicobar project, deeming it vital for India's national security and military presence. They argue it enhances surveillance of the Malacca Strait, crucial for checking China's growing influence in the Indian Ocean. The project is seen as a strategic and economic win.
articles.raw_summary: Defence veterans, including former IAF chief R K S Bhadauria, strongly back the Greater Nicobar project, deeming it vital for India's national security and military presence. They argue it enhances surveillance of the Malacca Strait, crucial for checking China's growing influence in the Indian Ocean. The project is seen as a strategic and economic win.

articles.title: Sudesh Kumar, hero of ‘Saranga’ and producer of ‘Uljhan’, passes away
articles.summary: Veteran actor and producer Sudesh Kumar, known for his roles in 'Saranga' and 'Chhoti Bahen', passed away at 95 in Mumbai. Kumar, who also produced thrillers like 'Uljhan', began his career in theatre before making a mark in films. His wife confirmed his passing after a brief illness. He was born in Peshawar and later moved to Bombay.
articles.raw_summary: Veteran actor and producer Sudesh Kumar, known for his roles in 'Saranga' and 'Chhoti Bahen', passed away at 95 in Mumbai. Kumar, who also produced thrillers like 'Uljhan', began his career in theatre before making a mark in films. His wife confirmed his passing after a brief illness. He was born in Peshawar and later moved to Bombay.

articles.title: Prasoon Joshi appointed as Prasar Bharati chairman
articles.summary: Prasoon Joshi, a noted lyricist and communications expert, has been appointed as the new chairman of Prasar Bharati, India's public broadcaster. The appointment fills a leadership vacancy and is expected to bring creative and digital expertise to the broadcaster amid its transition to modernize and adapt to changing audience preferences. Prasar Bharati oversees state-run media outlets like Doordarshan and All India Radio.
articles.raw_summary: Prasoon Joshi, a renowned lyricist and communications expert, has been appointed as the new chairman of Prasar Bharati, filling a crucial leadership gap. His extensive experience in advertising, cinema, and public communication is expected to bring fresh energy and a creative voice to the public broadcaster as it embraces digital transformation and evolving audience tastes.

articles.title: Jammu and Kashmir: Jammu bridge collapse toll rises to three, 2 engineers suspended
articles.summary: A bridge under repair in Jammu's Thuther area collapsed, killing three people and injuring two others—a rescuer and a worker. Authorities have suspended two engineers and formed a committee to investigate the incident. The tragedy raises concerns about infrastructure safety and maintenance protocols in the region.
articles.raw_summary: Tragedy struck in Jammu's Thuther area as a collapsed bridge during repair work claimed three lives. Rescue operations recovered the bodies after a section of the structure gave way. Authorities have launched a probe, suspending two engineers and forming a committee to investigate the incident and prevent future mishaps. One worker and a rescuer were also injured.

articles.title: Jammu and Kashmir: Warming Gulmarg turns to tech for four-season skiing future
articles.summary: Gulmarg's ski season is threatened by erratic snowfall due to warming winters. Authorities are accelerating plans to install artificial snow-making systems and synthetic slopes to extend skiing into all four seasons. The initiative aims to secure the resort's future and reduce dependence on natural snow. The Jammu and Kashmir administration is leading these efforts to safeguard Gulmarg's legacy as a premier skiing destination.
articles.raw_summary: Gulmarg's ski season faces an uncertain future due to shrinking winters and erratic snowfall. Authorities are fast-tracking plans for artificial snow-making systems and synthetic slopes to ensure the resort's viability. This initiative aims to reduce reliance on natural snow and extend skiing into a four-season activity, safeguarding its legacy.

articles.title: Oil marketing companies seek LPG, petrol, diesel price hike as Iran war deepens losses
articles.summary: Middle East conflict escalates, pushing crude oil prices above $126 per barrel and straining India's state-run oil companies. Facing mounting losses, these companies seek permission to raise pump prices, but the government hesitates, balancing consumer protection against financial pressures and potential inflation.

articles.title: Air India to cut nearly 100 flights till July: CEO Campbell Wilson
articles.summary: Air India is slashing nearly 100 flights through July due to soaring jet fuel costs and airspace restrictions, making international routes unprofitable. This move deepens the crisis for the airline, which already faces significant losses. The situation is exacerbated by global oil price shocks impacting India's oil marketing companies, with fuel prices surging dramatically.

The dispute highlights tensions between political parties and raises questions about free speech and governance.

articles.title: World War II-era unexploded shell found in Assam’s Tinsukia, defused: Army

articles.title: Explained: What is Mythos AI and why it is worrying banks and regulators
articles.summary: Mythos AI, developed by Anthropic, is an advanced AI tool that can quickly scan systems and identify vulnerabilities much faster than human experts. Its speed and capabilities have raised concerns among banks and regulators, who worry about its potential use as a weapon rather than just a protective tool. The article highlights the dual-use nature of such AI systems and the regulatory challenges they pose.
articles.raw_summary: Mythos AI by Anthropic doesn’t just find flaws, it acts on them. In hours it can scan whole systems and spot cracks that experts might take months to uncover. That speed makes it a powerful shield, but also a tempting weapon—and that’s what has regulators worried.

articles.title: Commercial LPG price hiked by nearly  ₹1,000 in setback for migrant workers, restaurants
articles.summary: The price of a 5-kg LPG cylinder, commonly used by migrant workers and students, has increased from ₹549 to ₹810.50. This sharp rise disproportionately affects low-income groups and small businesses like restaurants. The government has not yet announced any relief measures or policy changes in response to the hike.
articles.raw_summary: The 5-kg free trade LPG cylinder, used primarily by migrant labourers and students living near educational institutions, jumped from ₹549 to ₹810.50.

articles.title: Congress targets Himanta after SC relief to Khera; Assam CM vows further probe
articles.summary: The Congress party has criticized Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma after the Supreme Court granted relief to IAS officer Arun Khera, who had accused Sarma’s wife, Riniki Bhuyan Sharma, of possessing multiple foreign passports and undisclosed assets in the US. Sarma has stated that further investigation into the matter will be conducted. The dispute highlights issues of accountability and transparency in public office.
articles.raw_summary: Khera had accused Himanta Biswa Sarma’s wife, Riniki Bhuyan Sharma, of possessing multiple foreign passports and undisclosed assets in the US.

articles.title: Iran military official says war with with US ‘likely’ to restart as Trump 'not satisfied' with Tehran's proposal
articles.summary: An Iranian military official stated that a war with the US is 'likely' to restart after the US rejected Tehran's draft proposal. The proposal was delivered to the US via Pakistan. The tensions could escalate, potentially affecting regional security and global oil markets, which may influence India's foreign policy and energy strategies.
articles.raw_summary: The draft proposal was delivered to the US via mediator Pakistan on Thursday evening, the state media reported.

articles.title: Pilots’ body writes to DGCA over continued FDTL relaxations, cites safety risks
articles.summary: The Indian Commercial Pilots’ Association (ICPA) has written to the Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) warning that repeated exemptions to Flight Duty Time Limitations (FDTL) pose safety risks. The pilots’ body urged the DGCA to end these relaxations and ensure proper fatigue management across airlines. The letter highlights concerns over pilot fatigue and its potential impact on flight safety.

This ruling highlights legal proceedings in a high-profile case with potential implications for due process and civil liberties.

articles.summary: The UK Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) reported over 40 incidents involving merchant ships in the Strait of Hormuz between March 1 and April 27, including attacks, vessel damage, and harassment. This disruption could impact global oil supplies and shipping routes, raising concerns for India's energy imports and maritime security.

articles.title: What Is a ‘Margin of Error’ in Exit Polls and Why It Matters
articles.summary: Exit polls in India suggest varied outcomes for the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) and its allies in some states, while other regions show tight contests or continuity of regional parties. These projections could shape political narratives and potential policy priorities post-elections. The article explains the concept of 'margin of error' in exit polls and its significance in interpreting election results.
articles.raw_summary: Exit polls suggest varied outcomes, with projections indicating gains for the Bharatiya Janata Party and its allies in some states, while others point to tight contests or continuity of regional parties
